Description: Begins the real execution attempt using the current shared settings.
Opens route mode and start-direction settings.
Opens shared runtime thresholds and control settings.
Opens event-specific test selection and configuration.
Shows live accelerometer and gyroscope data from the built-in Nano RP2040 Connect IMU together with yaw and bias status.
Shows only the relative yaw angle; short press stores the current heading as the new zero/reference.
Opens the digital twin / execution-state monitoring screen.
Shows live IR state and shared IR-related thresholds.
Shows live ultrasonic readings and interpreted differences.
Selects how the next run chooses branches: sequence right left or selection.
Sets the initial robot heading used at the beginning of a run.
Pause time after stopping at a wall and before changing heading.
Front ultrasonic safety-stop threshold.
Maximum side distance that still counts as a wall for PID source selection.
Upper front-distance range that can still be classified as turn or junction geometry.
Front distance at or below which the robot can classify the path as a dead-end zone.
Front distance required before finish can be accepted when the finish-specific geometry also matches.
Time between repeated identical event checks during confirmation.
Number of identical event observations required in a row before the event is accepted.
How long the IR mask must stay unchanged before the code accepts the IR state as stable.
Proportional steering gain for wall-follow PID.
Integral steering gain for steady-state bias correction.
Derivative steering gain for damping and fast-response control.
Shapes small wall-error magnitudes before the PID controller is applied.
Desired side distance when only one reliable wall is visible.
Extra output scale applied when the final correction command points left.
Extra output scale applied when the final correction command points right.
Shows the wall-following PID interpretation screen for the currently selected heading.
Proportional gain for yaw-hold rotational compensation based on the angle between the saved and current IMU coordinate frames.
Integral gain for slow yaw drift that accumulates beyond the tolerance-based IMU deadband.
Derivative gain that damps fast rotational disturbances measured by the built-in IMU.
Additional angular threshold added on top of the propagated LSM6DSOX measurement uncertainty before yaw compensation reacts.
Shows saved and live IMU x-axis vectors
column7: the computed yaw error deadband and rotational command; short press stores the current body frame as the new initial coordinates.

Description: Same global event-verification gap used by execution and event tests.
Same global number of identical repeated events required for confirmation.
Same global IR stabilization time used before new IR states are trusted.
Shared dead-end distance threshold used during event determination.
Shared finish distance threshold used during event determination.
Sets the initial heading used before this event test starts.
Selects which branch to take when the forced test case is a T-junction.
Shared front stop threshold used when the handler approaches the wall before stopping.
Shared wall-detection threshold used for continuous PID source selection.
Shared upper front range that still counts as turn geometry.
Shared dead-end threshold used by the classifier.
Shared finish threshold used by the classifier.
Shared wait time before changing heading after the stop stage.
Shared time between repeated identical event checks.
Shared number of identical repeated events required before confirmation.
Shared IR stabilization time used before event checks trust IR data.
